title: search — model relationships
description: Five owned models with a hub-and-spoke shape: SearchIndex is the primary aggregate; SearchSyncJob + SearchSynonym hang off it. SearchSavedQuery + SearchAnalyticsEvent are user-facing peers scoped to workspace. Cross-module: SearchSyncJob may reference an xfer_artifact for the reindex-from-transfer-export flow.

internal.description: Sync jobs for this index. Cascade-delete with the parent index.
Optional index-scoped synonyms. Broader workspace + language synonyms have search_index_id = NULL.
Nullable — workspace + language scope when null.
Nullable — populated when the event originated from a saved query invocation. Retained for smart-list usage analytics.

outbound.description: Populated only when kind = 'reindex' AND source = 'xfer_artifact'. Reindex-from-transfer-export flow for cross-region migrations.
Polymorphic initiator. Typically User; may be System for scheduled reindexes.
Cascade drops private queries when owner is erased. Shared queries (is_shared=true) transfer ownership to a workspace admin via the AnonymiseSearchForErasedUser listener before the delete cascades.
Set to null on user erasure. Keep the row for anonymised aggregate analytics (GDPR Art. 17).

inbound.description: No other module holds an FK to search tables. Inbound integration is attribute-driven: any model in any module carrying #[Searchable] is discovered by the compiler at boot and registered in EntityRegistry / SearchIndex. HasActivityLog on SearchSyncJob + SearchIndex + SearchSynonym + SearchSavedQuery writes rows into activity_log (activity module) on their lifecycle events; HasAudit on SearchSyncJob + SearchIndex writes rows into audits (audit module) on the same signal — both are morphMany from those modules' perspective, not FKs on our side.
consumed_traits: laravel/scout::Searchable (wrapped by our own HasSearchable trait — applied to CONSUMER models via #[Searchable] attribute, not to search's own models)
